skill.name: rarefriend-skills
skill.description: Rarefriend skills help AI assistants work with a personal CRM, contacts, LinkedIn-synced networks, Google Calendar, Google Contacts, Microsoft Outlook email, and Outlook calendar through Rarefriend MCP.
skill.summary: Manage Rarefriend CRM contacts, notes, calendars, and email context from an AI assistant.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found most to be false positives from license text, markdown code spans, URLs, and setup examples. The confirmed risk is medium because the skills intentionally connect an external MCP package to sensitive CRM, contact, email, and calendar data. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Broad Access to Personal CRM, Email, Contact, and Calendar Data
medium_findings.description: The skills instruct the assistant to read and modify sensitive relationship data through Rarefriend MCP. Documented workflows include searching contacts, reading notes, listing Outlook emails, checking calendars, creating notes, and creating or canceling calendar events. This appears intentional for the product, but it requires strong user consent and least-privilege tool handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited workflows explicitly call tools that access or change contacts, notes, email, and calendar records. The use is consistent with the skill purpose, so the risk is privacy and permission scope rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Package Execution with OAuth Client Credentials
medium_findings.description: Setup instructions tell users to run the Rarefriend MCP package with npx and provide RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_ID and RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET as environment variables. This is a legitimate installation path, but it executes an external package and grants it access to user-authorized integrations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and environment variables are directly documented in setup files. No malicious endpoint is shown, but external package execution and credential handling are material operational risks.

low_findings.title: Local MCP Configuration Stores Secrets in User Config Files
low_findings.description: The setup guides place MCP credentials into local client configuration files such as Cursor and Claude Desktop configs. This is expected MCP setup behavior, but users should protect these files and avoid sharing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly show local configuration paths and credential fields. This is a common setup pattern, so the concern is secret exposure through local file handling, not hidden malware.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto, Backtick, and URL Alerts Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are from license or prose text, and the Ruby backtick execution alerts are markdown code spans and fenced examples. Hardcoded URLs point to project, documentation, or setup pages. I did not find evidence that these patterns execute by themselves inside the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the cited locations are license text, URLs, tables, and markdown examples. They do not provide executable source code or a direct command injection path.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx-Based MCP Runtime Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup path runs @rarefriend-ai/mcp through npx. Users should verify the package source and understand that the MCP server brokers access to connected services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is directly documented. It is a normal setup step, but it executes external package code.

dangerous_patterns.title: OAuth Integration Links for Third-Party Data Sources
dangerous_patterns.description: The skills instruct the assistant to create OAuth connection links for Google, Microsoft, Gmail, and other integrations. This is expected behavior but grants access to private data after user authorization.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The integration workflow and connectUrl behavior are explicitly documented. The risk depends on user authorization and downstream MCP security.

content.value_statement: Relationship context is often spread across contacts, calendars, email, and notes. This skill lets Claude, Codex, and other agents use Rarefriend MCP to search, organize, and act on that context.
actual_capabilities: Search Rarefriend contacts by name, company, role, email, tag, source, or exact match.
Read contact profiles, relationship notes, recent notes, and source labels.
Create and update contacts, notes, tags, and bulk contact imports through Rarefriend MCP.
Find free time and create, reschedule, search, or cancel Google and Microsoft calendar events.
Search Outlook emails and cross-reference senders with CRM contacts and notes.
Generate OAuth connection links for supported Google, Microsoft, Gmail, and contact integrations.
limitations: Requires Rarefriend MCP and valid Rarefriend OAuth client credentials.
LinkedIn sync depends on the Rarefriend Chrome extension or CSV import, not a direct LinkedIn API.
The skill provides instructions only; actual actions depend on MCP tools being available to the agent.
Users must review privacy scope before connecting email, calendar, and contact integrations.

use_cases.title: Prepare for meetings with relationship context
use_cases.description: Find a contact, review notes, check recent interactions, and summarize useful context before a call.

use_cases.title: Organize a professional network
use_cases.description: Search contacts by company, tag relevant people, add notes, and keep source labels visible.

use_cases.title: Schedule with contact-aware calendars
use_cases.description: Resolve attendee emails, find available time, create events, and log notes after the meeting.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Find [person name] in my Rarefriend contacts. Show their source, company, role, and any important notes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for a Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare me for my meeting with [person name]. Search contacts and notes, then summarize past context and follow-up items.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule a Contact-Aware Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule a [duration] meeting with [person name] next [time window]. Resolve their email, find available time, and ask me before creating the event.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Network Segment
prompt_templates.prompt: Find everyone I know at [company or segment], group them by role and source, suggest tags, and identify who needs a follow-up note.

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Acme, and what is the latest context?
output_examples.output: A concise list of matching contacts with source labels, roles, recent notes, and suggested follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: Find a time next week for a 30 minute call with Priya.
output_examples.output: A proposed meeting slot based on calendar availability, the resolved attendee email, and a confirmation request before booking.

output_examples.input: Search my Outlook emails about the contract and connect them to CRM notes.
output_examples.output: A short summary of matching emails, sender context from Rarefriend, and any relevant relationship notes.

best_practices: Check connected integrations before taking CRM, email, or calendar actions.
Confirm with the user before creating, updating, deleting, rescheduling, or canceling records.
Mention source labels when presenting contacts so users know where each record came from.
anti_patterns: Do not fetch every contact profile when a search result summary is enough.
Do not ask users to paste OAuth secrets or integration passwords into chat.
Do not schedule, cancel, or bulk update records without clear user approval.

faq.question: What services can this skill work with?
faq.answer: It covers Rarefriend CRM, Google Contacts, Google Calendar, Microsoft Outlook contacts, Outlook calendar, Outlook email, and LinkedIn-synced contacts.

faq.question: Does it require Rarefriend MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill depends on Rarefriend MCP and OAuth client credentials from Rarefriend settings.

faq.question: Can it read my email?
faq.answer: It can search Outlook email only after Microsoft email is connected through Rarefriend MCP.

faq.question: Can it change calendar events?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for creating, rescheduling, and canceling Google or Microsoft calendar events.

faq.question: How does LinkedIn sync work?
faq.answer: LinkedIn sync uses the Rarefriend Chrome extension or CSV import. The skill does not use a direct LinkedIn API.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a privacy warning. Users should understand the MCP package and connected integration permissions.
